The Public Health major is designed for those interested in working to improve health in diverse populations. The major will introduce students to the foundations of scientific and social knowledge relevant to public health. Students learn foundational concepts and approaches regarding health issues and their solutions, explore research methods, learn to think critically about upstream and downstream approaches in the field, and use case studies and problem based learning to define issues, assess populations, implement programs, gather evidence, analyze data, and evaluate efforts. The Public Health BA and Public Health BS differ in the amount of science preparatory coursework required.


Listed below are the lower division preparation courses for the major.


To be considered for this major, you must complete a MINIMUM OF THREE major preparation courses, by the spring before transfer:

  • One course in Cell and Molecular Biology or Life: Concepts and Issues,
  • One course in Chemical Structure or Chemical Principles, and
  • One course in Introduction to Statistical Reasoning or Statistical Methods for Life and Health Sciences.

One course in Communication or an approved Writing II course is STRONGLY RECOMMENDED.

All courses must be taken for a letter grade.

Applicants must have a minimum cumulative 3.2 GPA in all transferable coursework by the end of fall.


For more information regarding this major and UCLA's transfer selection process, visit: https://ph.ucla.edu and https://admission.ucla.edu.


PLEASE NOTE: the community college courses listed below have approved to satisfy the preparation for this major at UCLA, but they may not be exact equivalents of the UCLA courses.

","groupId":"c8b56f45-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":1,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralTitle","content":"AP EXAM CREDIT","groupId":"31b36845-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":2,"area":"General"},{"type":"GeneralText","content":"

The AP Exam Scores listed below may be used to satisfy the public health prep:

  • A score of 4 or 5 on the AP Chemistry exam can be used in place of the chemistry course.
  • A score of 4 or 5 on the AP Biology exam can be used in place of the life sciences course.
","groupId":"33b36845-6887-ef11-8474-000d3a37e3b6","position":3,"area":"General"}]
